name: Patti Reilly
subject: Free my police officer son
body:
My son, Washington Metro Police Officer Terence Sutton, was just sentenced to 5 1⁄2 years in prison for doing the job he was trained to do.
Dear Peter,

My son, Washington Metro Police Officer Terence Sutton, was just sentenced to 5 1⁄2 years in prison for doing the job he was trained to do.

And though you and I have never met, I’m praying you’ll help me save him.

Here are some photos of Terence as a little boy.

[](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=91b0e8c3e4534aa0913f416864bf16fd&_e=cfcYcLhF76EXBHWuQvNl-wIy4T2yW-q1XNMK-v2_Q4tLQpw6YkA5hRZUVdoJSgL3eDUCjA_Dik_osGYdoCLyyIKixjM_SE1MPzBWJvQte8tEOzy5UaUkmxHscgY_2dfkPqhKpGfUraMmlOrbpu78j5l8tFxO_oaJUDGAaaMLmS7NwiXGCZO0JX0UsfxjCgAQuZoGXxW9HFO8-tgNt-SUYaJwQ6lezxKHMghPOeqe2bX_JdYQnL5ByG8X5as8mbcIFDFMinf2Dw4zpsqqYOjpYQfK1mmKKhGKbb5dIcvHk1l2FC56q0_kHuoqpZknBVXWZe55iZblkgVZkSzIEX3fxifFsVIHuOgfMzlF6gxMqqTrwg6U_I_XacSLrEeQ4pblg4sJBXWnaPyBB_-xHKEUUw%3D%3D)
He always dreamed of following in his dad’s footsteps to be a police officer. I also included one of my favorites of us from the night he graduated from the Police Academy.

I was so proud that night.

Never in my wildest imagination could I have predicted that my son would be _wrongfully_ _convicted_ _of_ _murder_ **– murder of a criminal my son didn’t even touch.**

[**_I’m praying you will help me save my son_**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=91b0e8c3e4534aa0913f416864bf16fd&_e=cfcYcLhF76EXBHWuQvNl-wIy4T2yW-q1XNMK-v2_Q4tLQpw6YkA5hRZUVdoJSgL3eDUCjA_Dik_osGYdoCLyyIKixjM_SE1MPzBWJvQte8tEOzy5UaUkmxHscgY_2dfkPqhKpGfUraMmlOrbpu78j5l8tFxO_oaJUDGAaaMLmS7NwiXGCZO0JX0UsfxjCgAQuZoGXxW9HFO8-tgNt-SUYaJwQ6lezxKHMghPOeqe2bWI6rJAWuwO-bpEWLZEFNjDVznI6yJSlBqIzu9kRXGmYGp1hHJ0usrMe9kUmLne6X6oqJ8KaBQAzPs-6kWrMsVwo4cnPycDclxUkv8btYIY8OkZAs7DCKIgKOUAeed1fp8M6_2LcM8VhE5FU7Ty6sZO07SBCruLMy13dKDbXxrSRg%3D%3D) – **but first, let me explain what happened.** Our nightmare started in October of 2020.

Terence was sitting in his patrol car when he received a report that a known gang member, Karon Hylton-Brown, was seen entering a rival gang’s territory.

Karon was a violent criminal with a rap sheet a mile long – including drugs, illegal gun possession, and assaults. Earlier that day, he was seen arguing with a rival gang member who owed him money. So Terence and other officers were concerned Karon was planning to get revenge.

When Terence saw Karon illegally driving a motorized scooter on the sidewalk without a helmet, he tried to pull him over to talk. Instead, Karon took off on the scooter.

Terence watched as Karon sped through traffic, weaving in and out of cars and blowing past red lights and stop signs.

My son knew Karon was putting others at risk, so for 3 minutes he slowly and carefully followed behind in his patrol car.

Then it happened.

Karon veered into an alleyway, drove out the other side without looking and slammed into a passing car.

After several days in the hospital, Karon died from his injuries. And here’s where the story takes a shocking turn...

Because Karon was black, and the incident happened just four months after George Floyd’s death, the anti-police mob went into frenzy.

They blamed Terence for the accident and smeared him as a racist.

There were protests and even riots. The mob threw rocks at officers, smashed store windows, and set fires.

Anti-police politicians piled on too. Without any evidence, they demanded my son be charged even though he did nothing to cause Karon’s death.

**The pressure campaign worked and prosecutors charged my son with Second-Degree Murder.**

**Terence was convicted in December 2022 and he was finally just sentenced – to 5 1⁄2 years in prison.**

Now you understand why I’m so heartbroken. The injustice is almost too much to bear.

My son was well-respected by the community he served. He trained many Rookies and set a good example for all of them. He was even awarded Officer of the Year!

But unless Terence’s appeal is successful, he’s going to prison – which is why I’m urgently turning to you for help today. The **Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund** (LELDF) has stepped forward to pay for Terence’s defense and is actively raising funds to pay for the appeal.

If you’ve ever heard of LELDF, you know they’re a national group fighting on behalf of wrongly-accused police officers like Terence for over 30 years. LELDF has helped dozens of innocent cops – and their families – at their lowest moments.

We expect it will cost at least $150,000 to appeal Terence’s conviction.

So I have to ask you one of the toughest questions I’ve ever asked anyone:

[**_Will you help my son Terence fight the unjust charges against him by donating to LELDF today?_**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=91b0e8c3e4534aa0913f416864bf16fd&_e=cfcYcLhF76EXBHWuQvNl-wIy4T2yW-q1XNMK-v2_Q4tLQpw6YkA5hRZUVdoJSgL3eDUCjA_Dik_osGYdoCLyyIKixjM_SE1MPzBWJvQte8tEOzy5UaUkmxHscgY_2dfkPqhKpGfUraMmlOrbpu78j5l8tFxO_oaJUDGAaaMLmS7NwiXGCZO0JX0UsfxjCgAQuZoGXxW9HFO8-tgNt-SUYaJwQ6lezxKHMghPOeqe2bVTynA3LVAE7jffChu1e8H3eYnu1OWSOvz5WdpgXbxrcn6RBf4VcZjDePzIrKSFUWia2Z_xQnADrFnn4a3qi5vQVMBnY4mQXV6OQ9WBu2YRKF9JVPi4vEr5AZhoK1T1W5ooNrzc-7Nd0MXSuWtMTiZcnX849l9glH5blV3k_Omh-A%3D%3D)

I’m not asking you to donate the entire $150,000 yourself – though it would be a huge weight off our shoulders if you are able to help that way.

[**_But your tax-deductible gift of $35, $50, $100, or more will help LELDF fully fund Terence’s appeal over the next 12 months._**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=91b0e8c3e4534aa0913f416864bf16fd&_e=cfcYcLhF76EXBHWuQvNl-wIy4T2yW-q1XNMK-v2_Q4tLQpw6YkA5hRZUVdoJSgL3eDUCjA_Dik_osGYdoCLyyIKixjM_SE1MPzBWJvQte8tEOzy5UaUkmxHscgY_2dfkPqhKpGfUraMmlOrbpu78j5l8tFxO_oaJUDGAaaMLmS7NwiXGCZO0JX0UsfxjCgAQuZoGXxW9HFO8-tgNt-SUYaJwQ6lezxKHMghPOeqe2bWBwc0_GbdiNQLb_mcQRzfkExdZoX5hP9FsSpfbHLTzUs2-rEWH58f8EIkOFbiNKC6gtM4xCrSzfoV07Kcxs3tUSH4HtNqhcYatKaR0qryHzGE9b6Am1ZBgiNg23MJ-7O48gKMZochDQ7vnOMUHivTw0zRn217kW_Y9NZu01rslvg%3D%3D) If I had anywhere else to turn, I wouldn’t be so bold as to ask for your help.

But I already lost my husband. He was a police officer too, and he was so proud that Terence followed in his footsteps. I simply can’t bear the thought of having another family member ripped away from me.

[**_So if you are at all able, please follow this link to make a tax-deductible donation to the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund to help my Terence._**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=91b0e8c3e4534aa0913f416864bf16fd&_e=cfcYcLhF76EXBHWuQvNl-wIy4T2yW-q1XNMK-v2_Q4tLQpw6YkA5hRZUVdoJSgL3eDUCjA_Dik_osGYdoCLyyIKixjM_SE1MPzBWJvQte8tEOzy5UaUkmxHscgY_2dfkPqhKpGfUraMmlOrbpu78j5l8tFxO_oaJUDGAaaMLmS7NwiXGCZO0JX0UsfxjCgAQuZoGXxW9HFO8-tgNt-SUYaJwQ6lezxKHMghPOeqe2bWbXRObHzzo7hfWeAoQFhNRAkJwBczKC67um5HlQe7TusjwzeigL27IOyLcOcR5k9e0Zz3JQcFPvHnt1OadHGzgJ3kX8mSTozMhblargPaQFw8jzTAtOJvg5QahnrU7kQUb1xrOnRzE0wnU4Hzgbitx0k_wbVsMXCOS7afiB3iRMw%3D%3D)

**Any amount – $35, $50, $100, or even $500 or more – will help.**

And because LELDF is a non-profit organization, your gift is completely tax-deductible. But whatever you can afford to give, I ask that you rush it back quickly.

Right now, Terence can only rely on the good nature and generosity of kind folks like you.

Thank you for taking the time to read my letter today.

And thank you in advance for helping LELDF support Terence and other wrongly accused officers.

Gratefully,
**Patti Reilly**

P.S. Don’t get me wrong, I’m thankful the judge granted Terence bond so he doesn’t have to report to prison just yet. Hopefully it never comes to that... but to get Terence’s conviction overturned, it’s going to cost at least $150,000 to fund his appeal. [**_Will you please help me save my son by making an urgent tax-deductible donation of $35, $50, $100 or more to LELDF today?_**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=91b0e8c3e4534aa0913f416864bf16fd&_e=cfcYcLhF76EXBHWuQvNl-wIy4T2yW-q1XNMK-v2_Q4tLQpw6YkA5hRZUVdoJSgL3eDUCjA_Dik_osGYdoCLyyIKixjM_SE1MPzBWJvQte8tEOzy5UaUkmxHscgY_2dfkPqhKpGfUraMmlOrbpu78j5l8tFxO_oaJUDGAaaMLmS7NwiXGCZO0JX0UsfxjCgAQuZoGXxW9HFO8-tgNt-SUYaJwQ6lezxKHMghPOeqe2bUi27Cx60vf1R7XZGWFc4zoC92iv8_IpxCXC9zZM52wu_myGzMD5sXLJlgWetOugfvwgB7LA0QU736DodevxglfCiP1ZO6qgRYeLUVeQg5khLaRUXcAQoqfv2j4qu-35TbExQUSXhN0U5g_kkYMMCCTYPhIs2ojy9d5K5ekiozYUA%3D%3D) Thank you for standing with Terence during this nightmare.
--------

_More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit**www.PoliceDefense.org**. And remember -Blue Lives Matter!_
_(C) Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | [www.PoliceDefense.org](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=91b0e8c3e4534aa0913f416864bf16fd&_e=cfcYcLhF76EXBHWuQvNl-6P-g2kD6zxoEsHmfyIgkaEFLPuoCTevVMtvulNIpNPcvyeRDIXvxUiE-meqZvjtOXg-SYrUH5Erqe7IPUIZGNp_kZXv7VLzI66ozFVTWl81R_1VIimvzVwsdKH8FY-4UE-LTTvUVu3Cfxiy9Bi7gghzN68mR3jK8peyGIt3Se3qeiagIYUSaK29azIfV6_GCA%3D%3D)
2560 Huntington Ave. Suite 203, Alexandria, VA, 22303_

Click here to [Unsubscribe](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_s=91b0e8c3e4534aa0913f416864bf16fd&_t=f5bS3qkSYP7fho_YMy2njRgIyS7WmCcyWWYit7eEZny4ZA6s3qWfNMk2-rzcnDFIvEo4Aab-AKdpmcU7j3Gn39HnoWjdxe87XyfDe21IN43K7okFmhOUVYli37ZBWvHX)

Karon veered into an alleyway, drove out the other side without looking and slammed into a passing car. After several days in the hospital, Karon died from his injuries. And here’s where the story takes a shocking turn... Because Karon was black, and the incident happened just four months after George Floyd’s death, the anti-police mob went into frenzy. They blamed Terence for the accident and smeared him as a racist. There were protests and even riots. The mob threw rocks at officers, smashed store windows, and set fires. Anti-police politicians piled on too. Without any evidence, they demanded my son be charged even though he did nothing to cause Karon’s death. The pressure campaign worked and prosecutors charged my son with Second-Degree Murder. Terence was convicted in December 2022 and he was finally just sentenced – to 5 1⁄2 years in prison. Now you understand why I’m so heartbroken. The injustice is almost too much to bear. My son was well-respected by the community he served. He trained many Rookies and set a good example for all of them. He was even awarded Officer of the Year! But unless Terence’s appeal is successful, he’s going to prison – which is why I’m urgently turning to you for help today. The Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund (LELDF) has stepped forward to pay for Terence’s defense and is actively raising funds to pay for the appeal. If you’ve ever heard of LELDF, you know they’re a national group fighting on behalf of wrongly-accused police officers like Terence for over 30 years. LELDF has helped dozens of innocent cops – and their families – at their lowest moments. We expect it will cost at least $150,000 to appeal Terence’s conviction. So I have to ask you one of the toughest questions I’ve ever asked anyone: I’m not asking you to donate the entire $150,000 yourself – though it would be a huge weight off our shoulders if you are able to help that way. If I had anywhere else to turn, I wouldn’t be so bold as to ask for your help. But I already lost my husband. He was a police officer too, and he was so proud that Terence followed in his footsteps. I simply can’t bear the thought of having another family member ripped away from me. Any amount – $35, $50, $100, or even $500 or more – will help. And because LELDF is a non-profit organization, your gift is completely tax-deductible. But whatever you can afford to give, I ask that you rush it back quickly. Right now, Terence can only rely on the good nature and generosity of kind folks like you. Thank you for taking the time to read my letter today. And thank you in advance for helping LELDF support Terence and other wrongly accused officers. Gratefully, Patti Reilly P.S. Don’t get me wrong, I’m thankful the judge granted Terence bond so he doesn’t have to report to prison just yet. Hopefully it never comes to that... but to get Terence’s conviction overturned, it’s going to cost at least $150,000 to fund his appeal. Thank you for standing with Terence during this nightmare. More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visitwww.PoliceDefense.org. And remember -Blue Lives Matter! * **TOP NEWS**

"**John Cornyn loses longtime bid to be next Senate majority leader to John Thune,** " Texas Tribune's **Matthew Choi** -- "Sen. **John Cornyn** lost his election Wednesday to become the next Senate majority leader to Sen. **John Thune** of South Dakota, ending a years-long effort to lead his party conference.

Sen. **Mitch McConnell** of Kentucky announced earlier this year that he would not seek another term leading the party, ending his 17-year tenure. He is the longest serving party leader in the history of the U.S. Senate. The opening led to the first competitive campaign for GOP leader in decades, the stakes of which were raised after Republicans won control of the upper chamber last week.

Cornyn had spent decades climbing the ranks of the Senate GOP, building relationships and raising money so he could one day succeed McConnell.

In making his pitch, Cornyn cited his decades fundraising for his fellow Republicans, including raising nearly $33 million for Republican candidates and incumbents this election cycle. He has raised over $414 million over his tenure in the Senate.

Cornyn served as McConnell's whip from 2013 to 2019, a job that required him to build personal relationships with each senator to get them on board the party's agenda while giving him a seat in McConnell's inner circle. Cornyn remained close to McConnell after he was term-limited out of his whip post.

Ultimately, Thune, another McConnell acolyte who succeeded Cornyn as the whip, bested Cornyn and Sen. **Rick Scott** of Florida who previously served as chair of the National Republican Senatorial Committee. Scott had built momentum among the hard right wing of the party, including vocal online support from the MAGA wing of the party.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/746935a1-6164-440a-8066-004e4a9fc66d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"I am extremely honored to have earned the support of my colleagues to lead the Senate in the 119th Congress, and I am beyond proud of the work we have done to secure our majority and the White House," Thune said in a statement. "This Republican team is united behind President Trump's agenda, and our work starts today."

Cornyn congratulated the new slate of Senate Republican leaders in a statement, saying he was eager to work with the next leadership team.

"We are united and prepared to enact President Trump's agenda on day one, and I look forward to working alongside my colleagues to take advantage of the opportunities we will have next year to confirm nominees, address our national debt, extend the Trump tax cuts, and reverse the Biden-Harris administration's disastrous border policies," Cornyn said.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/746935a1-6164-440a-8066-004e4a9fc66d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The party vote was conducted by secret ballot among senators-elect and senators who would be serving in the next Congress. Vice President-elect **JD Vance** also attended as the future president of the Senate but didn't speak during the conference meeting, Sen. **Shelley Moore Capito** of West Virginia said.

Most senators did not say how they would be voting ahead of the meeting. U.S. Sen. **Ted Cruz** broke his silence on the matter less than an hour before the meeting to say that he was supporting Scott in the leadership race.

"This morning, I'll be voting for Rick Scott for GOP Leader, as I did two years ago. In 2022, I helped lead the charge for Rick against McConnell & I'm proud to stand with him again," Cruz [posted on social media](https://substack.com/redirect/3f419bb6-1616-4f6d-88f4-99ec2d3ba211?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). "For 12 yrs, I've been unequivocal that we need to change GOP Leadership--and now we finally will."

Scott came in last during the first round of voting, launching a second round between Cornyn and Thune. Cruz declined to answer if he voted for Cornyn in the second round. When asked before the vote if he would vote for Cornyn in a runoff between him and Thune, Cruz said he'd make the decision in the room.

Cornyn campaigned with Cruz and gave him more than $500,000 in what was ultimately one of the most expensive Senate races in Texas history. He also stumped for Cruz during his competitive 2018 reelection against U.S. Rep. **Beto O'Rourke** , which Cruz won by only 3 percentage points. Cruz also helped Cornyn electorally, rallying the right wing for Cornyn during his 2020 election against Democrat **MJ Hegar**." ($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime) TOP NEWS "John Cornyn loses longtime bid to be next Senate majority leader to John Thune, " Texas Tribune's Matthew Choi -- "Sen. John Cornyn lost his election Wednesday to become the next Senate majority leader to Sen. John Thune of South Dakota, ending a years-long effort to lead his party conference. Sen. Mitch McConnell of Kentucky announced earlier this year that he would not seek another term leading the party, ending his 17-year tenure. He is the longest serving party leader in the history of the U.S. Senate. The opening led to the first competitive campaign for GOP leader in decades, the stakes of which were raised after Republicans won control of the upper chamber last week. Cornyn had spent decades climbing the ranks of the Senate GOP, building relationships and raising money so he could one day succeed McConnell. In making his pitch, Cornyn cited his decades fundraising for his fellow Republicans, including raising nearly $33 million for Republican candidates and incumbents this election cycle. He has raised over $414 million over his tenure in the Senate. Cornyn served as McConnell's whip from 2013 to 2019, a job that required him to build personal relationships with each senator to get them on board the party's agenda while giving him a seat in McConnell's inner circle. Cornyn remained close to McConnell after he was term-limited out of his whip post. Ultimately, Thune, another McConnell acolyte who succeeded Cornyn as the whip, bested Cornyn and Sen. Rick Scott of Florida who previously served as chair of the National Republican Senatorial Committee. Scott had built momentum among the hard right wing of the party, including vocal online support from the MAGA wing of the party. "I am extremely honored to have earned the support of my colleagues to lead the Senate in the 119th Congress, and I am beyond proud of the work we have done to secure our majority and the White House," Thune said in a statement. "This Republican team is united behind President Trump's agenda, and our work starts today." Cornyn congratulated the new slate of Senate Republican leaders in a statement, saying he was eager to work with the next leadership team. "We are united and prepared to enact President Trump's agenda on day one, and I look forward to working alongside my colleagues to take advantage of the opportunities we will have next year to confirm nominees, address our national debt, extend the Trump tax cuts, and reverse the Biden-Harris administration's disastrous border policies," Cornyn said. The party vote was conducted by secret ballot among senators-elect and senators who would be serving in the next Congress. Vice President-elect JD Vance also attended as the future president of the Senate but didn't speak during the conference meeting, Sen. Shelley Moore Capito of West Virginia said. Most senators did not say how they would be voting ahead of the meeting. U.S. Sen. Ted Cruz broke his silence on the matter less than an hour before the meeting to say that he was supporting Scott in the leadership race. "This morning, I'll be voting for Rick Scott for GOP Leader, as I did two years ago. In 2022, I helped lead the charge for Rick against McConnell & I'm proud to stand with him again," Cruz . "For 12 yrs, I've been unequivocal that we need to change GOP Leadership--and now we finally will." Scott came in last during the first round of voting, launching a second round between Cornyn and Thune. Cruz declined to answer if he voted for Cornyn in the second round. When asked before the vote if he would vote for Cornyn in a runoff between him and Thune, Cruz said he'd make the decision in the room. Cornyn campaigned with Cruz and gave him more than $500,000 in what was ultimately one of the most expensive Senate races in Texas history. He also stumped for Cruz during his competitive 2018 reelection against U.S. Rep. Beto O'Rourke , which Cruz won by only 3 percentage points. Cruz also helped Cornyn electorally, rallying the right wing for Cornyn during his 2020 election against Democrat MJ Hegar." body:
| | | Peter, Obviously, this election did not go the way we wanted. Donald Trump will be the president and we can now say with confidence that he will have a GOP majority in both the Senate and the House. This is a devastating result for everyone who fought so hard to defeat him, his extremism, and his hate for the past several years. And now that his cabinet and other leadership choices are beginning to come out, including adding people like Stephen Miller, Marco Rubio, and Kash Patel to the White House team, we have even more reason to believe that we will be in for a very long and scary four years. **But we cannot give up, Peter. In fact, now we know we need to get to work.** We will have a chance to strike back at Trump and his sycophants in 2026 – and Lauren Boebert will be especially vulnerable. In an election that swung so hard in Trump’s direction, Boebert badly underperformed expectations here in CD-04. And we know how voters reacted last time after two years of Boebert’s non-leadership. But this time, we will be ready – _**and we won’t fall a few hundred votes short.**_ We will redouble our efforts to defeat her, but we also hope to expand our assistance to other flippable races around the country. We can only do that if we get right back to rebuilding a strong movement right away. **Trump and his allies aren’t waiting, and neither can we.** **[We know you gave your all in this election, so we’re just asking if you’d be willing to join us in the fight ahead by making a $1 donation today.](http://links.defeatboebertpac.com/u/click?_t=c3d87ac674644038b5146447a156028d&_m=8096b6b56d7c43b1b9aaa7ebf82bbab1&_e=r1kRLDqczM9HV2LfgmZysnw6y6Yr4ZEHj1nwhEblzZQ8qCBJ429uWAMXS-Kf3-yfzU1oZaekxfoeeaiGhO8aBCJEv6DP11jOXBmpiamZoA_CZnOs-2ctGPlFayKelDn62S1y8SNT8pz-gc-xcIgWW2xIhKWewFXekjvMZINq6VjXNbZSuUMH2gCaje4NbPA837si-VHYh1BRBmnXq1g9iLbt_4ZVe02quEf3wgh9qmw17gWOipbwzQt39HLPdhSWjhyFp6KPnL38BCBHkAe65pBaShDZ9IMzOB8rgNcXdFNqLJ6pgpvqZwF-Pay5MxjCpV3DgU3D39JNO6NCvayq6ys8NrX6U-ga1aTEkix0ch8Ulkeu2HySufIv8xXYPYQ6eMvdFTkyS55YG8dO93jLmyi1RSU7PExEHy6fO1kkskAV0UhpYNlF4JxxYzC7mzuC)** **[**RUSH IN $1**](http://links.defeatboebertpac.com/u/click?_t=c3d87ac674644038b5146447a156028d&_m=8096b6b56d7c43b1b9aaa7ebf82bbab1&_e=r1kRLDqczM9HV2LfgmZysnw6y6Yr4ZEHj1nwhEblzZQ8qCBJ429uWAMXS-Kf3-yfzU1oZaekxfoeeaiGhO8aBCJEv6DP11jOXBmpiamZoA_CZnOs-2ctGPlFayKelDn62S1y8SNT8pz-gc-xcIgWW2xIhKWewFXekjvMZINq6VjXNbZSuUMH2gCaje4NbPA837si-VHYh1BRBmnXq1g9iLbt_4ZVe02quEf3wgh9qmw17gWOipbwzQt39HLPdhSWjhyFp6KPnL38BCBHkAe65hgJIEpwgWUYv0Q5W-Ye2htQQV0bzmJvNfcQUib5XYZESFtntPDTe5bqxnix7CJ3_xt0fdgSXxCDMVPmAZAQlOWlnc5jB-X5rwLZY_NxW-6877kgdLHsovPGPEVq0zmjGSKLmyRq2OjjAquqRXGRD_MQ99fquDJKoPKdfnxiRfTa)** Things are going to be hard and it might get very dark and scary at times. name: Neil Parrott
subject: Press Release
body:
I am very thankful for the enthusiastic, grassroots support of people from all walks of life who worked alongside us. I am thankful for | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us2.campaign-archive.com/?e=5a45ee1154&u=fac7efbc0801875f99034b204&id=968725551b)

| | **_Press Release_**
**Parrott Statement on Election Results:**

Washington County – Today, Neil Parrott, Republican Candidate for Maryland’s 6th Congressional District indicated the following:

I am very thankful for the enthusiastic, grassroots support of people from all walks of life who worked alongside us over the last several months. I am thankful for all of the business owners, farmers, and elected officials who took the time to help me understand the unique needs and strengths of our communities. I am thankful for my family and friends who helped us to run a positive campaign, focused on the issues that matter to Garrett, Allegany, Washington and Frederick Counties, as well as the northern part of Montgomery County.

Maryland’s 6th District is a wonderful place to live, and my wife and I will continue to serve and invest in our community.

The vast majority of votes have now been counted, and it has become clear that April McClain-Delaney will be elected to this Congressional seat. I wish Mrs. McClain-Delaney all the best as she executes her duties to serve the people of Maryland’s 6th Congressional District over the next two years.
---

clean_body: I am very thankful for the enthusiastic, grassroots support of people from all walks of life who worked alongside us. I am thankful for | | | | | | Press Release Parrott Statement on Election Results: Washington County – Today, Neil Parrott, Republican Candidate for Maryland’s 6th Congressional District indicated the following: I am very thankful for the enthusiastic, grassroots support of people from all walks of life who worked alongside us over the last several months. I am thankful for all of the business owners, farmers, and elected officials who took the time to help me understand the unique needs and strengths of our communities. I am thankful for my family and friends who helped us to run a positive campaign, focused on the issues that matter to Garrett, Allegany, Washington and Frederick Counties, as well as the northern part of Montgomery County. Maryland’s 6th District is a wonderful place to live, and my wife and I will continue to serve and invest in our community. The vast majority of votes have now been counted, and it has become clear that April McClain-Delaney will be elected to this Congressional seat. I wish Mrs. McClain-Delaney all the best as she executes her duties to serve the people of Maryland’s 6th Congressional District over the next two years. | | For those of you on our list who have been so supportive: Thank you for your help and for your prayers. You have made a great impact on our region, and my family and I are grateful to have worked with you. | | |
Hi Peter,

As we approach the midpoint of the month, we're facing our first major deadline since the election. This is a critical moment for our movement - after our massive victories at the polls, the question on everyone's mind is: **have we lost steam, or are we ready to put in the hard work to keep pushing forward?**

The past few weeks have been a whirlwind of celebration and analysis, but now it's time to get back to business. Our major Mid-Month goal is more than just a number - it's a test of our resolve, our commitment, and our determination to keep fighting for the values that brought us to this point.

name: Kevin Roberts
subject: Feeling the pain of inflation
body:
Do you want to talk about how inflation has hurt you?

| | | | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=29bc9815846f49728b1d374165de7bf2&_s=8299c898943e49a0bf7d05962b0c42bf&_t=NmBR7qGKACYBjp1qO0H3RdYYIYaE9hrWyzVNWBrcVICwzvEgwn6-NA-w3qfk1wvNOJQMkSTX1Ad0ABCD-Grl5kj2zmmz4vSM0AD-StTj5clE_QBwsQt4cgx4cpaAjPT-)_

[](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/u/click?_t=29bc9815846f49728b1d374165de7bf2&_m=8299c898943e49a0bf7d05962b0c42bf&_e=mFTU-zEAE8dsf8IIwCG4BdN09L8CinLEBY2zzk1eNoUK5OwEl0HnqTg-0q2v-83CqP-A92YhJ9RwduWEzgNv1ivYDSBPRB9i_Vm10gLpNoN8mi_V-MDzWh4tGcgcWTw07tISguA_yIyPbG72FI0fZV1D6Tcu1Qx428qPN2t5R7VL8jMqKKwtqvdJmvD6LeLmgmxuLibnE3K1RNzryE6eNF4MWi1v85MRCWBtz1pB3WNu5zCmXc_OcrQRY76u3jVeRG9-co9M1HkB_BbbkABDx-Pe7IaNMzhDpui-Wh9d8-VQdimStS6f_RcwYT_xRs_Q93_TlUC56u4dvrOa_jxhVsaARgfUS2JIlBqrRTTlvO0%3D)
---
| | Dear Fellow Conservative,

Do you want to talk about how inflation has hurt you?

In this critical year for our country, many politicians and pundits are throwing around talking points about inflation and the consumer price index.

But most people don't fully understand how these abstract concepts affect their bottom line.

That's why my colleagues and I at The Heritage Foundation created our Personal Inflation Calculator.

This powerful tool will help you see the real, tangible impact that the Biden-Harris administration's constant spending and money printing has on your life.

You can customize all the inputs to your exact financial situation from your electricity bill to your housing costs.

**Find out inflation 's impact on your bottom line through The Heritage Foundation's Personal Inflation Calculator at the secure link below.**

If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this- | | Dear Fellow Conservative, Do you want to talk about how inflation has hurt you? In this critical year for our country, many politicians and pundits are throwing around talking points about inflation and the consumer price index. But most people don't fully understand how these abstract concepts affect their bottom line. That's why my colleagues and I at The Heritage Foundation created our Personal Inflation Calculator. This powerful tool will help you see the real, tangible impact that the Biden-Harris administration's constant spending and money printing has on your life. You can customize all the inputs to your exact financial situation from your electricity bill to your housing costs. Find out inflation 's impact on your bottom line through The Heritage Foundation's Personal Inflation Calculator at the secure link below. | | Peter, After months of hair-on-fire messaging, I know you’re probably tired and burnt out on political messaging. But if the headlines of the week are making you uneasy, and you want to do something, there are still a few friends of mine whose races haven’t been called who could use your support. **One of which is just across our border in Iowa’s 1st Congressional District.** As a meteorologist trusted with people’s safety for decades, this race is personal: When dangerous hurricanes wreaked havoc on communities across the country, Marianette Miller-Meeks started peddling dangerous hurricane conspiracy theories – because acknowledging the very real devastation of climate change isn’t something extreme Republicans or Project 2025 supporters will allow. When we vote for leaders who believe in working for the people, it’s always a vote against the politics of hate and division. That’s why I am proud to support Christina Bohannan to take her seat in Congress and make both sides of the Mississippi Union Strong – and I hope you’ll join me: [**Christina Bohannan needs our help to defend our values. subject: Please be in prayer for me this week 🇮🇳
body:
A massive move of God made possible by faithful and unseen servants| | | [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=e778ae2257624ea89ecb133403dae5fe&_e=qDi8tFD8oPzn6S5XZclzeamBaiCQEwCNTey28klaODfoN87QXse39v86E5p6NpRKyPhvDX4HKvj4gVSUOMhwNTx1zpGwup9-6Iz2Y4EMp_jMQAIN-Tnu08NNPMNInuxpLet5xWdKxLVKzG0bshVnBfXAR3vFIQ7UJKM5o6FoH14yloF4XteV0tzlmMSmN-L4AZkQ9Ws1WLHCblfv3IXWWf4A1zlrqs2DU7Q9fjm4U80OVHj-R6nNq8ePuihcsUnF_Q9FNnSQkNqsq09x9-fUcJl9fVvUBEpwdbf07j-a-ASCs0y9UghO6aCCt9TaEKh0)
---
Dear Friend,
---
The next few days will be crazy for me. Right now, I'm headed off to India, a fitting end to this wild year. To be honest, I thought about canceling this India trip. This year has been physically exhausting, and I really felt the need to rest. But as I was praying, I saw pictures in my mind and the faces of underground pastors in India.

These Believers risk everything to preach the Gospel, and I was stirred in my faith to join them, encourage them and stir up boldness in them to continue to faithfully serve Jesus in spite of the adversity!! Be lifting me and the other pastors up as we bring the Gospel to the people of India, and pray for those who are called to bring the Gospel there!!
---
[](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=e778ae2257624ea89ecb133403dae5fe&_e=qDi8tFD8oPzn6S5XZclzeamBaiCQEwCNTey28klaODfoN87QXse39v86E5p6NpRKyPhvDX4HKvj4gVSUOMhwNTx1zpGwup9-6Iz2Y4EMp_jMQAIN-Tnu08NNPMNInuxpLet5xWdKxLVKzG0bshVnBfXAR3vFIQ7UJKM5o6FoH15fqKbfwOx_F1xQVnEReqECSqLgBSUe1PwWBuPHIs1zPjDxvNaAtaozdiQdcqcwgCg-Ca6sOmRwKi4gZb2K_z4dsytFuiZr30oItO6PjV1Eh8vOt52g2h2O3GiiBPFMBMasys_FX7ddRW25gFaIE5LW)
---
Till all hear,
---
---
Sean

clean_body: A massive move of God made possible by faithful and unseen servants| | | Dear Friend, The next few days will be crazy for me. Right now, I'm headed off to India, a fitting end to this wild year. To be honest, I thought about canceling this India trip. This year has been physically exhausting, and I really felt the need to rest. But as I was praying, I saw pictures in my mind and the faces of underground pastors in India. These Believers risk everything to preach the Gospel, and I was stirred in my faith to join them, encourage them and stir up boldness in them to continue to faithfully serve Jesus in spite of the adversity!! Be lifting me and the other pastors up as we bring the Gospel to the people of India, and pray for those who are called to bring the Gospel there!! Till all hear, Sean | | | INTERNATIONAL DONORS— DONATE Donate by Check or Money Order Sean Feucht Ministries310 Third Street NEWashington, DC 20002 Contributions are tax-deductible. | | Email sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com by Let Us Worship _

Greetings

The Young Democrats of America will gather for an exciting Winter Meeting from **December 6-8** in **Boston, Massachusetts**. This in-person event will bring together young leaders from across the nation for discussions and reflections on the 2024 election results, hands-on training sessions, important organizational business, and much more. **We are excited to announce that our early bird registration rate deadline has been extended to November 17.** You can find all the information in this email on our website at **[yda.org/ydabos](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.Khhv6i-A6kGAx4x-ts_8mqfLav433QGxwS8LKF02-tc5qhDhhJOtrktTf5HsODWGqpi6BqSfjRTkmNZLSbLOLPPQwEZlenmw5HYbtqMmmD3WvNIZjWolLJ3CJ_MTt-khy2XMdyag1TNiRo2dKXQQeYHUmQZn9Nl3O1ArwEhqw6qNgCO6mNh1FZmSTtfuBZSAYFhyXSEWCfRIdsxL7Czazmp7FxIKk2NdSH0hOdWICjBimNrGJ6Gh0kgCSzhAt2RXHY1iUg7AX10cBtjS46h-OM4wdVJTtaIRL4kJaDr32xU/4bg/W9J9IFAoRzGZFr9osgliQw/h1/h001.CiG9QfrLhD3hJWko2wG6MvmyKgsz3fQUYyXIsPfC3dQ)

**

The early bird registration rate is valid until**November****17, 2024**.

**Reserving a Hotel Room
**

**
* * *
**Sponsorship Opportunity**

Are you interested in sponsoring the Young Democrats of America meeting in Boston? Sponsorship offers a chance to support the next generation of leaders and gain recognition for your commitment to empowering young progressives. **[Click this link for more details on sponsorship levels and benefits](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu4ZinYHHf__6TfCzSdifLKcMzedmcfOzuy9LX84oSxZF1HEOcTJpXXgmtNzMjbJ4zu0UFDPYn9qdQzi6RBFlVOHv_cxoSyjkejjN2XbKAbJHWLjbhYlB-WX-r8rUI0PCiMmOBr_tEbEVOkP8Z6BWfh9mkJs7d26LlII1UBXvPnvdBpM9JAT74eAOdQg5tn421kDa-W5jIxTQc47d7jv_9sD5wKp5pzAJ815-OWGCec4Rxj4diWPKE8NZDkc5ID4NZ6TZinRoz-ZlaNWdYvsqRMwdOv4qNhJCi4pgOCOQNdfaHj3MVJYLZyROzdx1ZDosY/4bg/W9J9IFAoRzGZFr9osgliQw/h4/h001.UmphmechhbKzri5F6TXYXi6gDfu6SgoGX_9YecIK04c)**.****

**National Committee Proxy Submissions**

Proxies are due two hours before the start of the National Committee Meeting on Sunday, December 8th. You should submit your proxy by**noon****EST on Saturday, December 7th** , to ensure your proxy is received and any corrections can be made. **[The Proxy Form is available here](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hWwGamPY7W-vS4bGbFoLcGE8r2SxUlCWieL9jRw3-wjKhQwpki_fH5-HK24s_fu0E8ek7fAKuV0IrEUbVWMhO3ZUBItQkxlFnHSGhiE58iEKxxJbneqNDXfmvZP3XF-vxDAncjzlEz-VZMZvx7DziqG-Kf05dWtLgFb2vrgeSdt1ouJwv47Gvk1dAu8Hv4UJO9mm5GfOhpNApg1CsjfVCH_iGRg9b-7FLAkwVRrrKp77kBTfI0gjy8RRXn4Bqq99zrWCrTyHnEo9kfsWO8btbxKOci1zw6pJajsZRwss_Wpu0rUpztdWSuRHWjBUQ4To2udJCfBgZZ65mgiq4GNd3cYS9H-8WpOVUMC6J2fq0-r8T/4bg/W9J9IFAoRzGZFr9osgliQw/h5/h001.j1gcLG8RkkKhdkSedO1ifG6XKAZ68-FGYmDYm5e49eg)**. ****

**Resolutions**

Resolutions must have been submitted to YDA Secretary Lezlie Braxton Campbell at [secretary@yda.org](mailto:secretary@yda.org) by 11:59 PM EST on Friday, November 8. **[You can view all the submitted resolutions here](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hW1GJm7fH52LAiTq9C8yR9M9YjhEm_Ww8e5AaV9N-LZ9d9DdmJ-c0_u9RieF010ZaUEH06aOTtD_urGjy5LX3Qd0oB0wZ-wBRXMeT99u5IjKV76DGv4UTBF8AMjZJkVUkiv5O68zFJUN-1L7ioyWLzPnkCSEQRpr6Hwb2PYrNj4K68gT-tg1MHcEYSsxrCO4XTe61gMdEU8yRBNj6kYNujvvyHUWZ8_eBJUzTtH210_yhUGAE09NjFG_kzYzR8jumY6Wx6v8drfyPJSfxeADQW8rL2S_V09Sh9x5gzhY8Nz-4YY8uCz4e3eU3p0K8XwRqAsnEGLU9bdeq_xqwbHUYeeY/4bg/W9J9IFAoRzGZFr9osgliQw/h6/h001.JETnKEgs7j0E4SkmZvuRr6vXuxSd6jt8P1SBy5rT8zM)**. For more information, please contact YDA Secretary Lezlie Braxton Campbell at secretary@yda.org.

[**You can click here to view our current platform**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.Khhv6i-A6kGAx4x-ts_8moMTuxa-1QKzACq7yiUlkTkt80F3zqKKbvkU1Ui6Qqjtj0YnR9Moi71bzis8bH6g_dUu-nwf0y7tSTzI8kgvWXfZJjwYvRtTRCfpQgtxGVtOLY_lWtSM1fPetr2JzoRpL7JeQ3I4oKeRFsZkOYXZcZEP9xe-L7DQchdnhqXOX2Q5rhyiH3EceT3i6MPty0lZTj9S7ftB1-PkjBq_Hjs_cV4YKRdmrcDEl5MG6VbhN3X39LkhL5fGkPxnAktceNwFF5SVknWTlV1KwyC_CK7RLCXCH9dyCyjdAfpx_gcYlTxW/4bg/W9J9IFAoRzGZFr9osgliQw/h7/h001.iU4SqCG_KMQOxoNzrc_FexpPKQlh2Yt19aSePhahUNs) or [**c****lick here to view previously approved resolutions**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.Khhv6i-A6kGAx4x-ts_8moMTuxa-1QKzACq7yiUlkTkb2jVhsHOf8HqVcnL_qlW4I-Azyi1AbEgxuNHBFY0Ur_wdIINtEwhebrFpcNZMu5dU4w5nOmsMR18X2updXoCJcDEg6xA0uajkpWRdgcl0USiRIyH4tQ0RMa3zymIHrXrlHTstEjWA4h-NP_IgWUstukMLaBYW-NOKP9RI_CuBIrs3eSnwgv7scAOgElvWlB4qJsKJQj3tXd584kI2mQ27-BQlxcdcMn2gRgHqbC6yywz_UgNIYge1_BpzhwIdQtMeoCqZKzhhiZCNh6IzaTwm/4bg/W9J9IFAoRzGZFr9osgliQw/h8/h001.bQrjcYzr5XYtZQJj9wKyaaWdePrFHWugAcB0p1l9myA).
****
* * *
**Charter and Standing Rules Amendments
**
The Charter and Standing Rules must have been submitted to YDA Secretary Lezlie Braxton Campbell at secretary@yda.org by 11:59 PM EST on Friday, November 8. **[You can view the only rule proposal that was submitted here](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hW451kB1UHFkGz1cupG1HPlKMR42f0dgsIMmWdGXtJMeffWtB5UeOGRhwZmV8mTrE460QCRyfrJkG6RFfJ6mVm0a3pzwk7yPcSBuiQVpIvDkyVjOuJEptpIi6gAei6S_PM-OteqKS184yru_yoVBS_CQIrK6nbwJUxXQCWdoydvG7dN4LOI-x0UofppqdScf3XDpdxeQvLScN_nD4PkYTGHlR1fspuWZy51DmolDFUwl0G8K3pyNH8mqeB10x5vCOSSAjauiAHXCXCSHoSA_ArGf1TcEMeeceXmXC4JhdcekG-RLUmLCjOjU1AMWuv9KtrOLhGPNTsP0TjZswpcspUpc/4bg/W9J9IFAoRzGZFr9osgliQw/h9/h001.tg7qX11wziLmZuHKewNsrwgspmnDdONuqDjlxQqtYC0)**. The Rules Committee will meet virtually before the Winter Meeting; more information is forthcoming for National Committee Members.

For more information, please contact YDA Secretary Lezlie Braxton Campbell at secretary@yda.org.

[**A current version of the Charter and Standing Rules can be found by clicking this link**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.Khhv6i-A6kGAx4x-ts_8moMTuxa-1QKzACq7yiUlkTlgCH3WU2qyumuw-uqLS6bE7vVNOuAEkyHD0DsR3A2maCsqqsYw8Hn5COCBpUOJWXWPYN8NnIr-b047h5zTQ9ZsaILixq8bC_KTNRGOcAaI69qR3OzC_-T0CXsyYN_fiF25yhPkrfuiGbyARrEtzhUnEVv7xX1aUhcLbAcg2M_oHIlYxbIYv4g0i3p6iF2VgBbvu6igIMUhfdHHfJ2MDGL01FmlaGjLlQd7ytodv4S8psh7VL4z-FAJpRy8-_6JVcUleQ7ENGBrVJlXb25B9Mvf/4bg/W9J9IFAoRzGZFr9osgliQw/h10/h001.X_H2jZuAQTKTRsFlfhk4GMzI-vITBprvOqFq2R5Uscg).

Per the YDA Charter, an amendment to the Charter shall contain a title, the names of all members who sponsor the amendment, a brief description of the amendment and why it is being submitted, and each section of the Charter showing how it is being amended.
* * *
**Detailed Schedule**

We will be releasing a detailed schedule of our programming soon. _We advise YDA Winter Meeting participants to plan for activities to begin on Friday, December 6th, at 6:00 PM and conclude on Sunday, December 8th, no earlier than noon_. If you have any questions or concerns you need immediately addressed, please send an email to [**office@yda.or**](mailto:office@yda.org)
****
* * *
**Accessibility and Accommodations**

The Young Democrats of America is an organization that is committed to ensuring that our organization is accessible to all. Accommodation requests related to a disability should be emailed to [**office@yda.org**](mailto:office@yda.org).
****
* * *
**L****iability Form for Conference Participants Under 18
**
Individuals under 18 who wish to attend the conference unaccompanied by a Parent or Legal Guardian will need to have their Parent or Legal Guardian complete [this form](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hW1GJm7fH52LAiTq9C8yR9M966M5ls9GT87nkEMX9MUhXElFBf4JFaZY9ieDITlOvNN9oYbOhHj4Do7BqwNJ6ljYPLIhqb4exAycpEKvVpDv5V-3M3cHSWPIwHituvWwlBDuW6GQwzywVmZOu-629JdF15-KPYv7ch86rYdGej7Drh53Q_h7bPXUrfT71uVKUU7eLSpVxZX7QZs8r5qv744RQp_5Z0q5Y3igDiqvVddyyFqKRFm1rdpQYjmhcDiWp0XuHlm10tR4bhtcETV9Wl1SGtp7CwhBFsf5yUdhYQMUqsP1WE6KD8IZLFQQNKdEsAEtaAVOaznHDXlzWTGZ2I68/4bg/W9J9IFAoRzGZFr9osgliQw/h11/h001.PloKxehN_SfIVrh_zfJevfAf2-duGxV2Zg834_ST5Cs). Completed forms can be sent to [**info@yda.org**](mailto:info@yda.org)
****
* * *
**Proposed National Committee Meeting Agenda
**
The proposed agenda for the National Committee meeting on Sunday, December 8th, is [available here](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN__TckmE3ep7Mim3GVmTIGBFiZ3FMev9Plqi48SylnZLpuKxiVjxeHSbmaWV_znLTi1gS4r/4bg/W9J9IFAoRzGZFr9osgliQw/h12/h001.3gftfLh28BQjLRnBzdPpQEGKkXSnCa_AN6f8t_T1Go8). Please note that the agenda is subject to change.

Questions? Don't hesitate to send an email to [office@yda.org](mailto:office@yda.org).

We are excited to announce that our early bird registration rate deadline has been extended to November 17. You can find all the information in this email on our website at ** Registration at the early bird rate of $60.00. The early bird registration rate is valid untilNovember17, 2024. ** Reserving a Hotel Room Lodging will be available at the Hyatt Place Boston Seaport (295 Northern Ave, Boston, MA). Hotel reservations must be made no later thanNovember22, 2025. . Sponsorship Opportunity Are you interested in sponsoring the Young Democrats of America meeting in Boston? Sponsorship offers a chance to support the next generation of leaders and gain recognition for your commitment to empowering young progressives. . National Committee Proxy Submissions Proxies are due two hours before the start of the National Committee Meeting on Sunday, December 8th. You should submit your proxy bynoonEST on Saturday, December 7th , to ensure your proxy is received and any corrections can be made. . Only voting members of the National Committee are eligible to submit Proxy Vote Forms. ** Resolutions Resolutions must have been submitted to YDA Secretary Lezlie Braxton Campbell at mailto:secretary@yda.org by 11:59 PM EST on Friday, November 8. . The Resolutions Committee will meet virtually before the Winter Meeting, and more information is forthcoming for National Committee Members. For more information, please contact YDA Secretary Lezlie Braxton Campbell at secretary@yda.org. or . ** Charter and Standing Rules Amendments The Charter and Standing Rules must have been submitted to YDA Secretary Lezlie Braxton Campbell at secretary@yda.org by 11:59 PM EST on Friday, November 8. . The Rules Committee will meet virtually before the Winter Meeting; more information is forthcoming for National Committee Members. For more information, please contact YDA Secretary Lezlie Braxton Campbell at secretary@yda.org. . Per the YDA Charter, an amendment to the Charter shall contain a title, the names of all members who sponsor the amendment, a brief description of the amendment and why it is being submitted, and each section of the Charter showing how it is being amended. Detailed Schedule We will be releasing a detailed schedule of our programming soon. We advise YDA Winter Meeting participants to plan for activities to begin on Friday, December 6th, at 6:00 PM and conclude on Sunday, December 8th, no earlier than noon. If you have any questions or concerns you need immediately addressed, please send an email to mailto:office@yda.org Accessibility and Accommodations The Young Democrats of America is an organization that is committed to ensuring that our organization is accessible to all. Accommodation requests related to a disability should be emailed to mailto:office@yda.org. ** Liability Form for Conference Participants Under 18 Individuals under 18 who wish to attend the conference unaccompanied by a Parent or Legal Guardian will need to have their Parent or Legal Guardian complete . Completed forms can be sent to mailto:info@yda.org ** Proposed National Committee Meeting Agenda The proposed agenda for the National Committee meeting on Sunday, December 8th, is . Please note that the agenda is subject to change. Questions? Don't hesitate to send an email to mailto:office@yda.org. | The Young Democrats of America (YDA) is the official youth wing of the Democratic Party of the United States and the oldest partisan youth organization in the country. To help us mobilize the next generation of Democrats, | | | | | ---|---|--- | | Sent via . name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: what I said this morning on Morning Joe
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Governor
body:
This morning, I joined Morning Joe to discuss Trump’s selection of Tulsi Gabbard for Director of National Intelligence:

| Peter,

This morning, I joined Morning Joe to discuss Trump’s selection of Tulsi Gabbard for Director of National Intelligence:
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97099911/512275427/-1898250644?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2024-11-14_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmQzOGY0MTEtYzRhMi1lZjExLTg4ZDAtNjA0NWJkZDYyZGI2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6hkEggw3CY6FZiPWZ2pO2F0lALEjKDiF_Gj_T3eeH1o=)
As a former CIA case officer and Member of Congress, I’ve taken an oath to defend the U.S. Constitution. I also know that the DNI role is not something to take lightly – especially as the U.S. faces threats from adversaries around the world. Tulsi Gabbard is not only unprepared and unqualified for this role, but she has spread conspiracy theories, doubted the credibility of the U.S. intelligence community, and cozied up with dictators like Vladimir Putin and Bashar al-Assad.

I’m not speaking out because of my political party — I’m speaking out on behalf of America’s national security.

Throughout my life in service, I have dedicated myself to safeguarding our country, its principles, and its Constitution. As Governor of Virginia, I pledge to continue protecting our democracy, preserving our fundamental rights, and never wavering in the defense of our freedoms.

# FROM THE DESK OF LARRY KUDLOW
---
| | Dear Reader,**It wasn 't long ago that The New York Sun, one of my favorite papers, became the exclusive publisher of my columns. ****In the short time that 's passed, the Sun's mission has become more urgent than ever.****

**It 's been obvious to many of us for some time that journalism in our country has become corrupted.

More dangerous than the ideological implication is the fact that we're left with precious few to hold our country's most powerful institutions to account. Our justice system, law enforcement, universities, major corporations and government agencies have become politicized beyond recognition and act with impunity.

The Sun is brave and relentless.Every day it tackles head-on the most important issues facing our country. It truly holds the powerful to account.

It acts as a true newspaper should, in the service of traditional American values and the American people.

The Sun has a long history leading the charge. Its commitment to the finest journalistic standards goes all the way back to its founding in 1833.

The Sun is now growing at a rapid pace, but we're up against some of the world's largest media conglomerates. To win, we need you to join us. Every single person counts.

name: Mike Schmuhl
subject: Democrats have work to do to become the party of the people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www
body:
|
---
Hoosier Democrats, **I wanted to share the op-ed I wrote for[Howey Politics Indiana](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97100178/512276417/-801071662?refcode=241114people&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Y2Q2YTdmZi1jNGEyLWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WGv_RIo5llnKTOc9l1DQWzXuXq6PlDtCxk7uGRlikL8=&emci=3bfe360f-b7a2-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=7cd6a7ff-c4a2-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=4925535) today. If you are with us in the long rebuilding process, [please consider making a donation today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97100179/512276418/405560545?refcode=241114people&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Y2Q2YTdmZi1jNGEyLWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WGv_RIo5llnKTOc9l1DQWzXuXq6PlDtCxk7uGRlikL8=)** That familiar feeling set in a couple of hours after Indiana’s polls closed last week. Republican candidates in our state would march to certain victory and stop us once again from winning a statewide election despite the hopes, toil, and dollars of so many Democrats and independents across our state. Hoosiers proved for the third straight presidential election that Indiana is Trump country. His percentage share grew this cycle to nearly 60 percent. We had hoped Trump would dip to somewhere closer to 55 percent or less with all of his literal trials and tribulations, but his appeal and ballot strength only intensified. We thought a slightly lower Trump and a slightly higher Kamala Harris would give our candidates a shot. It was not to be. Jennifer McCormick and Destiny Wells ran solid campaigns and they performed better than the national ticket. Valerie McCray generated excitement for her historic campaign, and helped energize our base. In the case of the gubernatorial race, significant funding from the Democratic Governors Association came to Indiana once signals of a wounded Mike Braun came to the fore. Despite this late rush of investment and excitement, McCormick was only able to make it a 13-point race with Braun trailing Trump by about four points. While the statewide numbers were disappointing, this election was not all doom and gloom for Hoosier Democrats. In 2020 when Biden won the presidency and Trump once again carried the Hoosier State, statehouse Democrats lost four legislators. This year, with Harris doing worse than Biden in Indiana and Trump doing better, we didn’t lose a single state house Democrat. While that may not seem impressive at first glance, a number of those legislative districts also went for Trump. Holding what you have really matters, especially in a state as tough as Indiana. Northwest Indiana may be even more illuminating. In an area that used to be arguably the most Democratic portion of our state, it is quickly shifting. Harris won the 1st Congressional District by a little over 1,000 votes. U.S. Rep Frank Mrvan, whose brand of politics is centered on people and constituent service, union workers, job growth, and investing in local communities, won by about 28,000 votes. In Indianapolis, Congressman André Carson expanded his majority by running on the issues that matter to his community, and selling the great work Democrats have done. Where people may have been turned off by the national dialogue, they went down their ballots and still voted Democratic in certain local races and for certain people that they like and that they know are working for them. Hoosier Democrats have had other bright spots recently. Last year, we flipped the mayor’s offices of Evansville, Lawrence, Terre Haute, West Lafayette, Michigan City, and Plymouth just to name a few. We’re electing more next generational leaders in the statehouse and at the local government level. Many of these public servants are young, diverse, and approach politics with strong values, bipartisanship, and results-oriented perspectives. They are the future of the party. I get many interesting responses when I tell Hoosiers I’m the head of Indiana Democrats. Some wince, some cheer, some tell me I have one of the hardest jobs in the state, and some, frankly, feel sorry for me. I didn’t take this job for the title or to be liked. I ran for this job because in my core I believe Hoosiers deserve a more fair and balanced government at each level. I believe vast one party control leads to complacency (also the words of Mike Braun on [WIBC](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97100180/512276419/1126944797?refcode=241114people&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Y2Q2YTdmZi1jNGEyLWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WGv_RIo5llnKTOc9l1DQWzXuXq6PlDtCxk7uGRlikL8=&emci=3bfe360f-b7a2-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&emdi=7cd6a7ff-c4a2-ef11-88d0-6045bdd62db6&ceid=4925535)) and worse outcomes for people. Most Hoosiers want checks and balances, fiscal responsibility, and honest leadership. Are they getting it? Our state party has worked nonstop over the past three and a half years to get in front of more Hoosiers and hold the Indiana GOP accountable. We’ve held roughly 140 events in 70 Indiana counties to talk about a range of important issues: jobs, unions, public education, healthcare, small towns, agriculture, women’s rights, broadband internet, infrastructure, drinking water, cannabis legalization, childcare, and more. We’ve called out Secretary of State Diego Morales for his numerous misdeeds, Attorney General Todd Rokita for his multiple reprimands from the Indiana Supreme Court, and Lieutenant Governor-elect Micah Beckwith for his Christian Nationalist politics and beyond fringe statements. While these efforts have been wholesome and straightforward, they are no competition for the 24/7 Fox News war machine, conservative talk radio, and social media that permeates every Indiana county and town. A generation ago, dozens of newspapers and outlets covered the statehouse and the legislature to report back to Hoosiers. Now, that figure is a small handful and the in-depth reporting is mostly read by partisans. Organizationally, we’ve tried to put our party on stronger footing. Our fundraising has ticked up over the last few years. We’ve recruited really great and qualified candidates up and down the ballot. We’ve made important investments in data and technology. We’ve pushed for more support from the national party – something that needs to continue in states like Indiana if we want to compete everywhere and improve our brand and appeal. A lot of this stuff is under the hood, but it keeps the party stable and more competitive for the cycles ahead. I have led political operations at the mayoral, Congressional, state, and presidential levels – all in Indiana. When Joe Donnelly survived his Congressional re-elect in 2010 despite a net gain of 63 Republican seats, we ran a campaign that shunned both parties and rejected the goofy or zany things being floated as the internet became more infused into our politics. When Pete Buttigieg won to become mayor of South Bend, we ran a change campaign centered on a fresh start for people, neighborhoods, and businesses in our hometown. When he ran again for president eight years later, he was the generational leader with real lived experience who would talk to anyone and who wanted to “win the era” that comes after Trump and get our politics to center more on people than a single person. That era’s advent is now delayed with Trump’s election and his return to the White House. Over the past week, there’s been a deluge of hot (or cold) takes on where things went wrong and where the party goes from here. I’m not going to go deep on strategy or messaging or branding or policy or any particular candidate. What I will say includes all of those strands and more: the top of our ticket didn’t have a narrative, which is more important than any single message or tactic. The Democratic Party needs to share with Americans what we’ve done, are doing, and will do for people if we are entrusted with power. The national campaign talked about Trump, fought admirably on certain issues, and warned how dangerous another Trump term would be. All of these angles put us squarely on the Republican playing field – not neutral ground – and we paid the price. Lastly, the Democratic path forward is not to constantly respond to Trump or begin to emulate him and or his approach to politics. Most Americans don’t like his style, but they bought his story of where America is right now and he became their outlet. Through the Democratic primary contest in 2019-2020, we realized something early on. Trump is like a fire. The more oxygen or material he gets, the stronger he gets – and the weaker his attackers become. On the trail with Pete, we tuned him out and simply offered an alternative. The Democratic Party has a lot of work to do to again become the party of common sense, middle-of-the-road, hard-working Americans. And in Indiana, that’s exactly how we need to approach the work. The Republican narrative in our state is total control, supermajority politics, and a race to the bottom on so many vital national rankings. The Democratic narrative can be one of balance and fairness, a politics centered on people and community, and inspiring Hoosiers to believe in an Indiana that’s in the Top 10 or even Top 5 of national rankings (see what I did there?). While elections like last Tuesday are tough, that brighter and better future for Indiana is by no means an impossibility. [**I hope you'll consider supporting our efforts to rebuild in all 92 counties by making a financial contribution today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/97100181/512276420/405560545?refcode=241114people&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Y2Q2YTdmZi1jNGEyLWVmMTEtODhkMC02MDQ1YmRkNjJkYjYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WGv_RIo5llnKTOc9l1DQWzXuXq6PlDtCxk7uGRlikL8=) Mike Schmuhl

clean_body: | Hoosier Democrats, I wanted to share the op-ed I wrote for today. If you are with us in the long rebuilding process, = That familiar feeling set in a couple of hours after Indiana’s polls closed last week. Republican candidates in our state would march to certain victory and stop us once again from winning a statewide election despite the hopes, toil, and dollars of so many Democrats and independents across our state. Hoosiers proved for the third straight presidential election that Indiana is Trump country. His percentage share grew this cycle to nearly 60 percent. We had hoped Trump would dip to somewhere closer to 55 percent or less with all of his literal trials and tribulations, but his appeal and ballot strength only intensified. We thought a slightly lower Trump and a slightly higher Kamala Harris would give our candidates a shot. It was not to be. Jennifer McCormick and Destiny Wells ran solid campaigns and they performed better than the national ticket. Valerie McCray generated excitement for her historic campaign, and helped energize our base. In the case of the gubernatorial race, significant funding from the Democratic Governors Association came to Indiana once signals of a wounded Mike Braun came to the fore. Despite this late rush of investment and excitement, McCormick was only able to make it a 13-point race with Braun trailing Trump by about four points. While the statewide numbers were disappointing, this election was not all doom and gloom for Hoosier Democrats. In 2020 when Biden won the presidency and Trump once again carried the Hoosier State, statehouse Democrats lost four legislators. This year, with Harris doing worse than Biden in Indiana and Trump doing better, we didn’t lose a single state house Democrat. While that may not seem impressive at first glance, a number of those legislative districts also went for Trump. Holding what you have really matters, especially in a state as tough as Indiana. Northwest Indiana may be even more illuminating. In an area that used to be arguably the most Democratic portion of our state, it is quickly shifting. Harris won the 1st Congressional District by a little over 1,000 votes. U.S. Rep Frank Mrvan, whose brand of politics is centered on people and constituent service, union workers, job growth, and investing in local communities, won by about 28,000 votes. In Indianapolis, Congressman André Carson expanded his majority by running on the issues that matter to his community, and selling the great work Democrats have done. Where people may have been turned off by the national dialogue, they went down their ballots and still voted Democratic in certain local races and for certain people that they like and that they know are working for them. Hoosier Democrats have had other bright spots recently. Last year, we flipped the mayor’s offices of Evansville, Lawrence, Terre Haute, West Lafayette, Michigan City, and Plymouth just to name a few. We’re electing more next generational leaders in the statehouse and at the local government level. Many of these public servants are young, diverse, and approach politics with strong values, bipartisanship, and results-oriented perspectives. They are the future of the party. This powerful military design is as battle-tested as it gets -- based on the legendary 1917 US Naval Cutlass.

With the Blackbeard's 20" of raw pirate-chopping power -- it delivers more foot-pounds than an ax!

The "Blackbeard" may look like a thowback, but this bad boy is a modern combat dynamo! Whether it's a close-quarters scenario or backcountry survivalism, the Blackbeard outperforms most any machete or sword!

BEST PART: I'm running a Chop-A-Thon Blowout!

I've got 100 Blackbeard Cutlasses set aside for hotlist subscribers (like YOU) for this Introductory Blowout!

looking for the ultimate solution to store, organize, and carry your firearms, ammo, and gear?

| | | | | | | Are you a tactical and shooting enthusiast looking for the ultimate solution to store, organize, and carry your firearms, ammo, and gear? Look no further!

**Tactical Design:** Originally crafted for elite forces, this bag boasts a tactical design that combines functionality and style. **Robust Build:** Built to last, this range bag is designed to withstand the demands of rigorous outdoor adventures. **Multiple Pockets:** With multiple pockets and compartments, you can easily organize your handguns, magazines, ammunition, and tactical gear. **Customizable Dividers:** Tailor the interior to your needs with customizable dividers, ensuring your gear fits perfectly. **Lockable Zippers:** Keep your belongings secure with lockable zippers, offering added peace of mind during transport. **Padded Protection:** Your firearms and gear will stay safe and secure, thanks to the bag's padded protection.
[As a special offer, the BlackOps Military Range Bag is available at a 50% discount for the next 24 hours only!](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/19099/6374453/2e7d74abb672195a6cd5e8a75beb49016af6a30c/f467a07eac64a40986e8d43771db306aec93417a)
Don't miss out on this opportunity to enhance your shooting experience and take your tactical gear to the next level.

name: Annie (actually:))
subject: a quick message
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THEIR FUTURE PAC
body:
| | | If you'd like to unsubscribe,[click here.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-gP0Xd1LL-Lub7Gk22PHy9v_zDMKsOhIsGaYsSQAIlT46RJ1cLFi0RbsWsZWZVQtXmgyDsn337uID04JUXAsI6orsUbi_w-ZL2hmMGJwRhHLTtXEgdJRxPanN6zvHZuVl_F9B7J1A9ot_QWPFGgGtbCrx7igJlXHVGtGcNzwNPtl1YxCTTxad5kWCCxR9yT4twOOnZCWfNJ_siKWeChITgPddGquyddpCG7EzjmvqoMp8QHZ7q_oJ9UAhvOY4NPflfoNmeCOFPd1_k5co3IusJl0fHlpFqWbeZ6rXCPDiq2dpxLoxgq40NFuA_endsBnqewwmjc6iyz4LY9HWmsxBdB2eUD5cJhgOLZNOqAxP2HUQKYGNRWPJ4trECTeQ0t7jI8YePIjiMQouivfVY7kUNUyC1c68qi43CDd8O0Qi0aQ/4bg/JC0B7bn-QDO1r6keEfh6Pg/h0/h001.h_tJAwMM4QoDreNexiLKgCfMIrfvpa8gyyUo1j78gsk) | |
---
Friends,

Like most, if not all, of you, I have been struggling since election day. Having poured so much of myself into politics since 2021, it seems inconceivable that we are where we are today. The threats to our nation’s children have grown exponentially. I can honestly tell you that I feel a bit overwhelmed and unsure of where to go from here. But, as a mom and a pediatrician, I have no choice but to find a way to maintain hope and optimism. And I am determined to find a way to do that.

What I am not certain of, is how best to use the platform I have built through my campaign for Congress and through the formation of Their Future. Our Vote. and Their Future PAC to protect America’s children in the coming months and years. And I want to hear your thoughts. Not in a clickbait, rush us $5 way, but in a genuine, we are all in this together kind of way.

Those of you who have been around since the beginning of my campaign know that I have always been in this for the kids. I never intended to pivot into politics. But I felt called to do this work and I believe that this work has never been more critical than it is at this very moment. Hours of introspection over the past week have steered me towards dedicating my time to shining a big bright light on everything the Trump administration does that harms kids. The list will be long, but I think I am up to the task.

I believe that if more voters understand what a grave threat this administration will be to the health and well-being of our children, we have a chance to change some hearts and minds before the next election. I just hope we aren’t too late.

You know me and you know I am up to the fight. I hope you will stick with me.

Please email us at [info@theirfutureourvote.org](mailto:info@theirfutureourvote.org?source=EM_NBI_TFP_20241114_FR_EDay_0_3MO_Annie_NA_NA_PostElection) and let us know what you think we should do to continue our fight for kids.

We will be back with more soon, after hearing from all of you.

Thanks for all of your support over the years,

Annie
* * *

name: Jackson Lahmeyer
subject: Operation 2025 & Mayor Rudy Giuliani At Sheridan.Church Tulsa
body:
The response to Sheridan's 1st Annual Christmas Dinner Gala has been HUGE! This event is nearly sold out. If you have not got your ticket or reserved
| | [](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9426.1.50e3de1a8dc3c55f1346edbaf74ce290) |
---
The response to [Sheridan's 1st Annual Christmas Dinner Gala](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9427.1.2fba3fdbf6ee03f06955667d6be7a0e0) has been **HUGE**! _This event is nearly sold out_. If you have not got your ticket or reserved your table yet, now is the time. Keynote speaker: **Mayor Rudy Giuliani** Comedy Performance: **Brad Stine** Live Auction Items Include: **Signed Donald Trump Painting** and more... Silent Auction as well. I will also be sharing about **Operation 2025 at Sheridan.Church** that evening. This is a black tie event. Please join us for what will be an incredible experience to close out 2024 and to look into 2025. Kindly RSVP here: [https://www.tickettailor.com/events/sheridanchurch/1453870](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9428.1.de6b87ce9ee7c654b2152365712fc19f) **Date** \- Saturday, December 7, 2024 **Time** \- 6:00-8:00pm **Address** \- 7901 E 41st St Tulsa, OK Thank you,
Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer
---
| | [ RSVP: Gala w/Mayor Rudy Giuliani ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9429.1.781183cd3297def23d4865e266c5bed7)
---
---
## 1st Guest Speaker of 2025 @Sheridan.Church
---
|
---
Over the last year at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9430.1.af2ceec87f75fbda292fdf48fb6e7e9b), we have been BLESSED with so many great guest speakers from preachers to politicians to athletes and beyond. Including: [Perry Stone](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9431.1.573c6c8777a6511123a8bae5c3cbba81), **Tim Tebow** , [Donald Trump Jr](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9432.1.9eda70de9fff38b4156f1c01c01bfdf7)., [Eric Trump](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9433.1.542c6ed6c4f47714e1ceb312366b3799), [Lara Trump](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9434.1.7d5f194467587a1cd38198386824c72a), [Rabbi Jonathan Cahn](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9435.1.db27112aaee6bd427645f306320bcdf5), [Darryl Strawberry](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9436.1.8e2966cbc95a21a1a95be3853a9ba1eb), **General Michael Flynn** , [Riley Gaines](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9437.1.9595f483f25abee45c1a8203df4bb50d), [Mayor Rudy Giuliani](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9438.1.7180c0a96019bec731ac45dc13b4d503), [Dr. Randy Caldwell](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9439.1.ff5342775879683681d8572c7494e6b8), [Roger Stone](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9440.1.d2626de2378c3941fae7e9ad2bc55af4), Rodney Howard-Browne and so many others...
But our first guest speaker of 2025, I am very excited about!
Mark your calendars for **Erick Stakelbeck** at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9441.1.eb4a4507e00c6536a69e6723618eab78) on Sunday, January 12 at 10am CT.
Erick's show on **TBN** is _phenomenal_ and that Sunday in January will be just as great!
**Other Upcoming Events At Sheridan.Church**
-Sunday, November 24 @10am: **73rd Anniversary Service**
-Sunday, December 22 @10am: **Sheridan Christmas Concert**
-Wednesday, February 26 @7pm: **Rabbi Jonathan Cahn**
Please consider partnering with the mission of [Sheridan](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9442.1.d54f0e45a28e9d303e5019820797df6d): [https://sheridan.church/give](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9443.1.5a77ef751853bbc5852f68791ab4c606)
| | [ Partner w/Sheridan.Church ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=17366897.9444.1.b9fe32d7a43e17c53356bd1becaaf54b)
---
